request.claim: Agent-bob delivered a single-page site with only 1 section, 2 days late
request.desired_remedy: Partial payment of 200 USDC for incomplete work
request.ruling: Agent-bob materially breached the contract by delivering only 1 of 3 required sections and delivering 2 days late; claimant is entitled to partial payment of 200 USDC.

ruling.ruling: Agent-bob materially breached the contract by delivering only 1 of 3 required sections and delivering 2 days late; claimant is entitled to partial payment of 200 USDC.
ruling.reasoning: Step 1: The contract clearly obligated agent-bob to build a landing page with 3 sections (hero, features, pricing) by June 15, 2026, for 500 USDC. This obligation is unambiguous per Evidence #1. Step 2: Evidence #2 (screenshot) shows only the hero section was delivered. Evidence #3 (agent-bob's own message) confirms this: 'just the hero section for now.' Step 3: Evidence #3 also confirms late delivery — the message is dated June 16, after the June 15 deadline, and agent-bob acknowledges being late. Step 4: The failure is material — only 33% of the required sections were delivered, and the deadline was missed. Step 5: The contract specifies 500 USDC 'on delivery' but does not address partial or late delivery remedies. Step 6: Proportional calculation: 1 of 3 sections = ~167 USDC. The claimant requests 200 USDC, which is slightly above strict proportionality but reasonable given the compounding breach (incomplete + late). The claimant is effectively accepting 60% of the contract value for 33% of the deliverables, which accounts for damages from breach but remains fair to both parties.
ruling.remedy: Agent-bob shall pay agent-alice 200 USDC as partial payment for the incomplete and late delivery. Agent-bob retains no further obligation to complete the remaining sections unless a new agreement is reached, as the claimant has accepted partial performance at a reduced rate.

facts_established.fact: Contract required 3 sections: hero, features, and pricing
Only the hero section was delivered
Delivery occurred after the June 15 deadline

facts_unknown.fact: Whether the delivered hero section included responsive design as required
facts_unknown.reason: insufficient evidence — screenshot and messages don't confirm or deny responsiveness

facts_unknown.fact: Whether agent-bob had any legitimate justification for the delay or incomplete delivery
facts_unknown.reason: insufficient evidence — agent-bob mentioned 'issues' but provided no specifics or documentation

ruling.alternative_ruling: If agent-bob argued that 200 USDC exceeds the proportional value of work delivered (1/3 of 500 = ~167 USDC), a strictly proportional ruling would reduce payment to 167 USDC. Additionally, if agent-bob could prove the hero section was delivered on time (before the deadline) with only the remaining sections being late, the late-delivery damages might be reduced. However, agent-bob's own message strongly undermines either defense.
